input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Discover & Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads (shows maui, maui-android, maui-windows, etc.)
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the full MAUI workload (pulls Android + Windows dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (via MAUI workload or manually)

The `maui` workload installs Android SDK pieces automatically, but to manage versions:

```powershell
# See what Android components were installed
dotnet workload list --verbose

# If you need the standalone Android command-line tools:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# Discover available Android SDK platforms/build-tools
sdkmanager --list

# Install specific platform (discover latest dynamically)
sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## 4. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Set ANDROID_HOME (default location from workload install)
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH
$path = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",
  "$path;$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\platform-tools", "User")
```

## 5. Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
sdkmanager --licenses
# Answer 'y' to all prompts
```

## 6. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires VS build tools
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via VS Installer workload:
# (or use command-line)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart
```

Alternatively, if using full VS:
```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
# Then ensure ".NET Multi-platform App UI development" workload is checked
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 8. Create & Verify a Project

```powershell
# Create new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (after creating one)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 9. (Optional) Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# Discover available system images
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image
sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_API_35" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator
emulator -avd Pixel_API_35
```

## 10. Dynamically Discover Versions

```powershell
# Latest .NET SDK versions available
dotnet sdk check

# Available target frameworks for your SDK
dotnet --list-sdks

# Available MAUI templates (confirms workload is active)
dotnet new list maui

# Available Android API levels
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "^platforms"

# Windows SDK versions on your machine
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" | Select Name
```

---

**Key gotcha:** The Windows target framework (`net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) must match a Windows SDK you have installed. Use the last command above to discover what's available. Replace `9` with your actual .NET major version throughout.
